channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Saylor sold 3,588 BTC — 41 replies — 164.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Saylor sold 3,588 BTC

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-07-06 18:19 UTC

OP

Saylor sold 3,588 BTC

Early replies (first 3)

62450542
such a comedy

buy high, sell low

mutts are cuckolds, france would conquer them in two weeks

62450550
>>62450537
Where Michael? Where?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62450749
>H-Here's why it's actually GOOD thing that Saylor is selling, Chud!
Imagine actually trusting Saylor somehow who spent literal decades bagholding after the dot com crash?

62451001
>V activated
we're so back

62451003
They're making the point that their BTC can be sold, and it can be sold without causing a death spiral, and they will sell if their debt and credit obligations requires this.

Notice how the market response was already much weaker this time.

I don't particularly like this sale, but you bullshitters made him do it by pretending all BTC bought from STRC proceeds is essentially lost because he could never possibly sell any of it.

62451011
Just $1 billion left to sell

62451013
>>62451003
The point is well made even though it does make them look retarded for buying high and selling low. If you are going to be 'rebalancing' then have a trading strategy already.

62451019
>>62451003
>and it can be sold without announcing that it was sold until after a three day trading weekend with low enough liquidity that bits can wash trade the price up then dump on retards the following Monday

62451036
>>62451001
>babby's first dead cat bounce

62451039
>>62451003
>buy at 120K
>sell at 60K

see guys, the company is doing the needful

62451042
>>62450537
Honestly impressed how well the market absorbed this. Bullish as fuck. Death spiral believers on suicide watch

62451044
All it takes is Strategy bot buying for the bottom to drop out.
Actively selling is death.
Not sure why people still thing your average retard and third worlder is propping up the market at this level.

62451049
>>62451042
>this is fine

62451053
>>62451039
His average acquisition price is $63k, no biggie

62451081
>>62451053
fuck off saylor

62451088
>>62450537
Paper handed bitch

62451132
>>62451039
Who cares whether they sell a tiny amount at a loss?
Your type will also celebrate unrealized losses, then disappear when the company will inevitably sit on unrealized gains again some time.
These metrics are meaningless in a treasury like this with no liquidation price, and which is massively over-collateralized. You only want to distract with your useless garbage.
>>62451081
You piece of shit started out claiming he bought at $120k, the other guy is at least closer. Per FIFO accounting we could also look at his oldest coins purchased in 2020 around $10k, in which case he just made a 500% profit. That's another useless distraction in your style.

62451164
>>62450537
DO NOT REDEEM SAAR

62451185
>>62451132
oh man you seem emotionally invested

enjoy holding the bags i guess

62451188
>>62451132
A 500% profit, well after 50% inflation more like 300%. That's why we use average, and that is 75k. Now factor in inflation and we are at 85k at the very least. Still quite a loss to sell at 60k.

62451234
>>62451185
You are useful in the sense that a crowd of your kind dominated the price action in the past few weeks, so even a very superficial reality check showed that Strategy was on sale, and you contributed to that discount.
I did load up those past two weeks, yes. Now that I'm done, I'm getting a little tired with these rumors, half-truths and other parroted garbage because I've read enough of it. I don't think of myself as particularly emotional, you can tell yourself that if it makes you feel better.

62451251
>>62450537
saylorcoin is FUCKED till this cuck is out

62451267
>>62450537
>unlimited dividends forever

62451294
>>62450537
He sold? Pump it

62451302
>>62451234
To the chair you go, cuck!

62451550
>>62451036
>>babby's first dead cat bounce
HAHAHA

62451591
saylor bought every top and now he is going to sell every bottom?

62451626
>>62450537
we goin 2 da mewn baby

62451629
>>62450537
https://x.com/Overdose_AI/status/2074139052563980439
What a shithead btw.

62451634
HE SODLED

62451659
>>62450537
Kinda funny that the dividend payments are 2x more than just taking out a loan on your bitcoin.

62452014
>>62451044
>Shitcoin seller says shitcoin needed, more at 8 pm!

item.title: my brother-in-law is a literal genius, like 150+ IQ, and worked his ass off over the last 15 years or so to become a suc… — 38 replies — 152.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
my brother-in-law is a literal genius, like 150+ IQ, and worked his ass off over the last 15 years or so to become a suc…

Open thread • Replies: 38 • Updated: 2026-07-06 18:17 UTC

OP

my brother-in-law is a literal genius, like 150+ IQ, and worked his ass off over the last 15 years or so to become a successful entrepreneur and just completely failed. he's flat broke now. it's crazy to me how hard it is to make good money even if you're smart and hardworking. the world is fucking brutal.

Early replies (first 3)

62449875
>>62449873
“Smart” and didn’t go all in crypto. I shiggy diggy

62449876
Almost like being a business owner is just being a wagie with more work and all of your capital at risk.

62449881
>>62449876
Nah that's being self employed, an entrepreneur convertes other peoples time and labour into money

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62449926
>>62449909
this is also true. relying on extrinsic outcome motivation is a 100% guaranteed way to an perma incel doomloop with the end-stage being suicide

62449942
>>62449909
Name one successful poojeet franchise

62449948
>>62449942
This is more white incel loser cope

>waaaah anyone who calls out my crybaby behavior is an Indian, a foid or a joo

Grow up

62449954
>>62449873
If he gives up after one failure he's not going to make it through the next 5 failures that will eventually teach him how to succeed

also geniuses don't usually do great at business. The sweet spot for starting a business is in the 100-120 range. Smart enough to make good decisions but dumb enough not to give up when things go wrong anyways.

62449984
>>62449948
Lol poojeet fails to name a brand and seethes

62449992
>>62449984
Not him but that one pajeet was smart with providing Indians with free internet which then destroyed the internet for the rest of the world.

62449994
>>62449992
Steve poojobs? He did look a little bit indian at the end

62450060
>>62449873
He's probably too smart. He want to create great things that revolutionize peoples lives, but the easiest way to make it is to scam stupid people.

62450077
>>62449984
Why would a poojeet take orders from a white incel loser? Y'all lower caste than dalits. Know your place and lower your tone

62450101
>>62449873
I got paid a full day at my job on Thursday for LITERALLY doing nothing but writing my magnum opus novel, watching my niece and playing pokemon showdown.


It's a skill issue. Not an IQ one.

62450119
>>62449876

If you work “in” the business it’s just like owning your job.

62450121
>>62449942

Tata motors which owns Jaguar and Land Rover.

62450123
>>62450101

You have a job tho, it’s different than trying to start a business

62450129
>>62449887
top silicon valley ceos are all high iq, top wallstreet executives too, there's just no way you can survive that kind of environment without constantly observing obscure patterns, so your shallow analysis doesn't track
>>62449873
your brother in law probably failed because he wasn't part of the group, he probably tried to muscle into someone else's turf, what business was he into?

62450177
>>62450121
I know of someone who own a land rover, a newer model. He went for a drive in sand with it and the engine burned out, he got it replaced and went down to the beach to drive over sand dunes. Engine burned out again

Lmao aryan quality, I didn't know tata acquired land rover in 2008 but it makes sense now

62450181
>>62450077
You took your vaccines like a good cattle everyone knows you poojoos are the most subservient minions

62450429
>>62450077
Castes aren't a real thing it's some turdjeet larp among themselves.
In reality there's only turdjeets and actual human (white) people.

62450441
>>62450429
Face it you white incel losers are the lowest caste people in the world. Unlike Indians NO ONE likes you or wants you around AND you are BROKE

62450451
>>62450441
Get off that white dick poopoid

62450460
>>62450451
Post some more about how you are broke and depressed but deserve an easy life because you are white

62450481
>>62450460
I deserve an easy life and I have an easy life while you're broke and depressed and looking for some dick

62450485
>>62449873
I'm retarded and have made millions.
Just the way it goes sometimes OP.
Being smart isn't a ticket to success crazy enough.

62450500
>>62449873
BTDT. I was lucky that Bitcoin came along when it did or I'd be living in a cardboard box sucking off meth addicts.

62450584
>>62449873
>150+ IQ
>worked his ass off over the last 15 years
158 IQ here. Only one of those statements can be true (probably the 2nd). If he were actually that smart he'd have known how to optimize things so he didn't need to work his ass off. I've been loafing about the last few decades and my liquid net worth is just over $4m having barely lifted a finger.

62450615
>>62450584

This is where we mention there is autistic "intelligent", and then there is intuition. Some people can solve anything you put in front of them but can't inductively synthesize a shrewd or creative idea. AI brain, basically lol.

62450621
>>62449887
High IQ overthink, underact, avoid risk taking
>AAAHH! I have tooo thiiiiiink first
Low IQ underthing, overact and dont even register risk

62450636
>>62450584
Wanna invest in my super secret club?

62450659
Naybe OP isn't.smart enough to recognize whether somebody os a genius. What if the brother in law is actually a dumb ass and has people fooled?

62451127
>>62449873
These bots think they are posting their demoralization threads on reddit now

62452004
>>62449873
making it is more about emotional regulation than about iq past like 110 or so

item.title: BEARISH ON COSTCO — 43 replies — 109.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
BEARISH ON COSTCO

Open thread • Replies: 43 • Updated: 2026-07-06 18:05 UTC

OP

People are scamming costcos lenient return policy now. Should we short COST?

Video:
https://x.com/Basedgoymemes/status/2073860759269077199

Early replies (first 3)

62449396
based

62449400
I wouldn't call jeets 'people'

62449441
Fuck this racist, post the person who started to do this was some white bitch, she did it for 12 years

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62449769
>>62449716
Make me

62449770
i would take my old furniture to costco for no other purpose than creating a physical obstacle for boomers trying to shop

62449829
>>62449441
Bloody brown bitch yes

62449846
>>62449769

62449850
>>62449770
But you dont. Why? are you lazy or a coward or just a blowhard incel?

62449862
>>62449388
Izzat status?

62449895
>>62449850
I'm a lazy coward blowhard incel but at least I'm making decent money from my investments.

62450126
>>62449846
kek

62450247
SAAAAAAARS

62450251
>>62449388
>Buy gold at Costco.
>Return when price gone up.
>Free money

62450253
>>62449388
Wtf is wrong with indians?

62450259
>>62450251
?
You would get the same money that you paid when you bought it you fucking mongoloid.

62450296
>>62450251
Can’t return gold

62450328
>>62449441
Go back, Sukdeep

62450386
>>62450253
Why did you let them take over?

62450507
>>62449388
Just slit his throat if you're the customer behind. This is a fixable problem

62450508
>>62449716
>Poodia
It's called Fecalia, anon

62450519
>>62450507
>COSTCO Anonymous
White incels demanding someone else do their man work

62450613
>>62450251
Leave it to the goyim to exchange literal gold for literal paper and think they're getting one up on you.

62450617
>>62450613
Only morons think gold us a good investment

62450619
>>62449388
stock prices have nothing to do with how profitable a company is

62450628
>>62450251
Brown poojeet IQ in display. Receipts exist.

62451091
>>62450617
good goy

62451501
>>62451091
Ngl you should port only a fraction of your wealth in boomer rocks. There’s always a small chance asteroid mining isn’t a meme and boomer rocks does a -99% overnight.

62451725
jugaad

62451765
>>62449441
shut the fuck up jeet, you aren't even potty trained plus your people actually eat cow shit

62451803
>>62450251
Can't return gold.
BUT if you buy it online and don't sign for it twice it automatically goes back to Costco and you get a refund (source, happened to me).
So you could use this for speculation on gold- if you buy it and the price drops before you receive it just don't sign for it.

62451853
>>62451765
You are jelly of our success

62451899
>>62451853
Cope

62451948
>>62451899
Indians are winning

item.title: Why do people act like buying a house is a good investment >Property taxes >Maintenance >Repairs because something WILL… — 12 replies — 48.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why do people act like buying a house is a good investment >Property taxes >Maintenance >Repairs because something WILL…

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-07-06 18:17 UTC

OP

Why do people act like buying a house is a good investment
>Property taxes
>Maintenance
>Repairs because something WILL go wrong
>Even if you're "beating inflation" with a mortgage you're paying much more than its actual cost
>Closing costs
>All just so you can sell it (likely at a loss when considering inflation) so you can buy a slightly less expensive house
It's not that buying a house is a bad purchase if you can afford it, but acting like it's somehow an "investment" is absolutely retarded, you'd probably make more back from buying Pokemon cards

Early replies (first 3)

62450381
>>62450377
You're supposed to buy houses on a discount
>t. Selling for double what I purchased in 2023 after $20K in upgrades LOL

62450391
>>62450377
Idk I just want one to live in, but maybe they rent it that way person who rents pays bills and when he break something you can tell him to pay for it and then when they don't give a fuck anymore they sell it

62450397
>cbf moving every year because the landlord decides they a wankers and want to sell to some Chinese immigrants who took our jobs

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62450401
>>62450377

You really wanna know? For most people it’s because owning a house forces you to save via the mortgage payments. Yes, you could put that money into index funds instead or whatever and come out ahead, but normies wouldn’t do that, they’d spend it. For a single /biz/tard buying a house is probably not a good investment. But for most people, especially families, owning a house will improve their financial position over time because they will be forced to build equity over time instead of spending that money on stuff.

That’s why normies say housing is a “good investment”. For them it’s better than the likely alternative.

62450434
>>62450381
>double
you could've just bought MU, SNDK, etc.

62450436
It can work out cheaper than renting, depends on prices, now is probably a bad time to buy. No doubt all kinds of privilege taxes will be heaped on home owners too in the coming years, I think it's smarter to buy property outside of the west, the benefit also is that you can leave to live somewhere else at any time

62450445
Great, another rentoid cope thread

62450446
>>62450377
It’s hard to lose money from owning a house. What are the alternatives? Either rent where you get no money back, or if you’re lucky enough to live with parents who don’t ask for anything. Tax is a non issue. One year of tax is the equivalent of a renter paying one months rent. Maintenance is virtually cost free. Cleaning up the house is what any responsible human being does regardless of whether they own a house or not. Repairs happen anywhere. Learn to DIY. You shouldn’t need to spend $400 for someone to replace a $40 faucet that takes 30 minutes to do. It’s not only about investing from a financial point of view either. Owning a home is more piece of mind and being able to do whatever you want (not counting HOAs). That alone is worth it more than anything else

62450464
Renting when old will be a massive issue in years to come.
Pensions will not cover rent AND living for the vast majority of people.
It's an issue not reared its head yet because boomers own homes and have pensions that cover their living, or they are selling their homes for care.
Unless you know you are going to be wealthy, you need to own a home at some point or be utterly doomed to hardship in an already strained system in your old age.

>inb4 i'll just rope when old LULZ
Childish nonsense talk.

62451291
>>62450377
>Property taxes
>Maintenance
>Repairs because something WILL go wrong
all of this is wrapped up in your rent payment dumbass. you really think those costs don’t exist for your landlord and he’s not passing them on to you, plus profit?

62451450
buying a house cured my depression
best investment ever

62452006
when the market crashes everyone will still own their homes and will have enjoyed living in them but you'll own nothing and will have been living in an apartment listening to blacks keep you awake all night all that time

item.title: A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn… — 11 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn…

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-07-06 18:25 UTC

OP

A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn $1 per 1 LINK per DAY through staking. Does that mean if I’ve staked 2000 LINK, I’d earn $2000 per day?
Is that true, or was the anon just talking bullshit?

Early replies (first 3)

62448322
>>62448316
Just bullshit. At 4% annually your link would need to be worth $9125 per token.

62448378
>>62448316
He is just talking bullshit. They just pushed all their users to pay through stripe. There is not really a pathway to imagine corporate successes translating into token holder value. Payment abstraction layer and staking were both a flop and they were what link holders always said would be the way they would make it but now instead they deleted the past marketing materials, disallowed comment on it in their AMA and pushed Stripe payments. It has unironically never been more obvious and is so obvious it is borderline in the exit phase

62448411
>>62448322
10k Link eoy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62448505
I wish.

62448514
>>62448322
>>62448378
Kek why do fuddies always flag themselves as newfags by replying to pasta. Retarded fuddies. Just bought more LINK, seethe and dilate.

>posted from my iPhone from my beach house in Mexico

62448520
>>62448514
Not fudding - I bought another 1000 this week. What's your vision for where this is going?

62448521
>>62448316
if you follow the money there's a good reward waiting for those who stake

62450512
bump

62451573
>>62448520
I'm a cuckold and I just LOVE holding Chainlink. Whenever Sergey dumps millions of tokens on Binance I get super hard! Fuddies can cope and seethe but this is my fetish and you can't kink shame me

62451657
he didnt pay his ladyboy

62452033
>>62448514
Been here longer than you. I bought link in 2018 with early biz and sold when it pumped and it was clearly not building out for token holders alongside literally all of early biz before the necrotic, tech illiterate, petrified fossil of late normie greed cultists flooded in to fill the cavity and be the findom submissive of Sir Gay who sells to them with zero prospect of value return for token holders, even selling node operator rewards with the network unable to support 30% of function through revenues. You are not a smart investor, you literally just get hyped about cheap "announcements" which mean nothing for token holders whatsoever and are literally written to seem like they do with zero actual technical reality so that they can use them to sell tokens to you to fundraise for the company and salaries. Your analysis amounts to "price went up here before and I missed it but I won't miss THIS time because it will TOTALLY happen again, just..cuz!! Oh, did you see the news on x institution using CLL for price feeds?! I am going to be SO rich! No, I don't understand how this will impact actual token demand or how the token will fit into the equation at all, but the announcement is from Chainlink Labs, so it must somehow!!!"

item.title: Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news? — 11 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news?

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-07-06 18:16 UTC

OP

Seems like every BTC pump or dump is blamed on ETF flows or macro headlines.

>Is that just the reality now, or are people overlooking other factors?

Early replies (first 3)

62450753
>>62450728
Its 100% cz dependent, just tradingview has to write something to the price action.

62450760
>>62450728
Btc does this thing where it lulls the newfag into a false sense of superiority. Both in bull and bear markets. In a bull market, it tricks newfags into thinking it will go up forever and traps them in overleveraged longs and in bear markets, it tricks them into it never going back up and they either sell low, sell covered calls or lever up short and get milked. Just hold spot and don't pay attention for a decade.

62450902
>>62450753
cz?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62450909
>>62450760
In short = manipulation

62450916
>>62450902

62450937
>>62450916
Wait....what??

62450950
>thinks news drives the market

62450961
>>62450760
>just hold spot
>t. baggy
If you bought Bitcoin at the end of 2017 and held for 9 years, you would currently have no more value against gold than when you bought.

62450971
>>62450937
>not knowing who cz is

62451749
>>62450971
CZ is just another clown

62451999
>>62451749
A hardcore clown

item.title: Day 354 of snailposting every day until LINK is at $10 — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Day 354 of snailposting every day until LINK is at $10

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-07-06 18:14 UTC

OP

Day 354 of snailposting every day until LINK is at $10

Early replies (first 3)

62451040
>>62451028
baaaaased and cuckold pilled

True marines are willing to hold for another 10 years. We always knew it was a twenty year hodl.

PRAISE KEK
77777

62451048
>We always knew it was a FOREVER AND EVER hodl.

nobody is getting you old outdated crap bags!

62451566
>>62451040
>>62451048
fuddies working OT today

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62451577
>>62451566
Based and praise kek my fellow Link marines. Those dirty fuddies want to steal your precious Link tokens, don't fall for their FUD.

Praise kek
shadilay!

62451649
>>62451577
nobody wants you shitlink

62451937
>>62451028
Soon we will be flapping on the wings of a butterfly! Or is that a caterpillar? I forget (just kidding)! Someone call Bill Nye the Based Science Guy to help sort this out! Actually we probably don't need to call him because he is for sure a linkie, he's SMART!!!!!!! GO GO GADGET LINK INVESTMENT!!!!

62451988
>>62451937
repeat after me

we will NEVER sell
we are warriors
we are Link marines

item.title: Will SPCX pump, since the all ETFs tracking the NASDAQ-100 will be forced to purchase stock? — 6 replies — 23.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Will SPCX pump, since the all ETFs tracking the NASDAQ-100 will be forced to purchase stock?

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-07-06 18:13 UTC

OP

Makes sense at first, but it will be such an artificial pump, I bet most will sell their shares in SPCX. It better go to 80-100$ per share.
+
It seems like Elon wants to fuse TSLA with SPCX, which could also make it pump. Now I am wondering if this company will have the same track record as AMZN. At first they also had no revenue in 1999, but then exploded in value.
Your thoughts?

Early replies (first 3)

62451856
spcx cant be profitable since they merged xAI in it, just abandon the ship it's not worth it

62451863
>>62451856
AI is the reason SPCX is worth so much because of data satellite launches

62451870
>>62451849
It’s gonna pump SO HARD

most /biz tards have no clue how good SpaceX AI is now since they acquired Cursor

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62451871
>>62451849
> I bet most will sell their shares in SPCX.
Idk what you mean by this. Only 4% of shares are trading right now. The first lockup period for another 20% doesn’t happen until August when the q2 financials are released.
I do expect a pump tomorrow, but the gains will be wiped out when the first unlock period happens.

Right now spacex is vapor ware. Starlink is great, but it’s a small part of the company. The actual rocket part and xai are both hemorrhaging money

62451962
>>62451849
Yes it will pump, but mostly from spculators front running the forced buying. After 9:30am tomorrow the forced selling will have stopped, the speculators will be exiting their positions and, who's going to be buying then?

62451979
>>62451962
The forced buyers need to buy $64 billion in shares, thats 60% of the float

item.title: Index dogs justify your life — 9 replies — 20.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Index dogs justify your life

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-07-06 18:02 UTC

OP

Adjusted for inflation, half of the decades the S&P 500 yields ZERO return.
You could buy the S&P 500 each month for the next 20 years, and it wouldn't be out of the ordinary if at the end of these 20 years you would have had ZERO returns - adjusted for inflation.

Early replies (first 3)

62451667
>>62451639
So i should exit now and get back in in 2040?

62451701
>>62451639
I mean, it's still worthwhile as an inflation hedge and therefore the base of your portfolio. But yeah, an index fund won't make you rich. It just allows you to save your money. The only way to make money is by picking and trading individual stocks, which is beyond what most people can do while maintaining their full time job.

62451785
>voofaggots convinced me not to buy ai stocks
>this was 8 months ago and now they're up 800%
Never trust voogroids.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62451805
>>62451639
>years arbitrarily chosen to capture major stock market depressions
>neglects to mention that the point of dca is to keep buying at the lows as well
You're retarded if you fall for this obvious demoralization.
Keep gambling on memes and stay poor forever, niggers.

62451807
>>62451639
Wow when things are good they’re good and when they’re bad they’re basically just 0 and not negative
This is so bad, you should’ve known what to short during 08 instead or perfectly hedged against the dot com bubble in another way baka

62451824
>>62451639
0 stress
Guaranteed line go up
Just dollar cost average in over time
Beat 95% of hedge funds
It's literally the best move for 99% of humans.

62451829
yes a bear market happens once a decade
yes we are probably due for one
but I'll continue to participate. it could go up 30% from here before going down 20% in a bear correction.

62451922
>>62451701
Thats retarded tho, these companies are getting a free ride basically. You should refuse to index invest on principle because these incompetent fucks cant make money

62451938
>>62451922
I roundtripped the gold run up, got 60-80% gains on the recent chip bull and had the presence of mind to sell.

Then I was feeling frisky and took a small position in 2xDRAM etf and got smoked down 30%. said fuck this and put everything in VOO. I’m done researching everyday and envisioning my theses playing out and adjusting fuck this shit

item.title: WTFWT — 2 replies — 2.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
WTFWT

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-07-06 17:30 UTC

OP

W T F W T
T
F
W
T

Early replies (first 3)

62449036
>>62448983
Short liquidations for a while now. You cannot just let it fall every single day, so the goyim will short it down to the ground all night and win!

62451818
OOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. — 69 replies — 93.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job.

Open thread • Replies: 69 • Updated: 2026-07-06 17:44 UTC

OP

I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. I'm not young, 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

Early replies (first 3)

62449979
I live in California, too.

62449982
What did you do for the last 23 years? Hard to help without more context. Do you have skills?

62449989
>>62449982
Depression and NEET means not in education employment or training.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62451161
>>62451093
>jews want 40 year old losers to work
>jews spend time online giving sage advice to 40 year old losers online
I don't even think he's jewish. Anon may be a leftfag but his advice is pristine. Get some fucking discipline and transform yourself.

62451241
>>62449961
>How fucked am I?
Approx. 100%. Nobody will ever want to hire you with a 20+ years gap in your resume. I know because i'm just like you, just 2 years younger. But at least i used my free time to invest and run side gigs so i managed to get myself a $200k cushion for when my parents croaks. It's not perfect but it will leave me some time to figure things out, because i'll always be on my own, nobody will ever hire me, and they'd be right, i'd do an absolutely atrocious wagie.

The question is : why now? Did your parents die and didn't leave anything behind for you to survive on?
You have to put yourself in the employers' shoes : would you hire yourself if you were the boss? If you are honest the answer will be no. So you'll have to open your own business if you want to survive. And you can do quite well outside of the wagecuckery loop. Personally i'm insanely lazy which is why i'm a poorfag, but per hour i earn much more than the average wagie.
The only issue is you'll need a little bit of capital to get started, how much do you currently have in savings?

62451269
You keep acting like I did something wrong. I did all the hoop jumps I was supposed when I was younger. Nobody wanted to hire me then. I gave up for a while, the depression or whatever hit. But that wasn't my fault, I needed help but all I got was abuse. Just like this thread.

62451296
>>62451269
Yeah, life is a bitch, but you are supposed to have figured this out in your 20s, not at 40. Still, not everything is lost, but you have to change your mentality. I guess you watched a shitload of animus in your free time. Do the MCs keep whining and bitching? Or do they keep struggling against all odds until they achieve their goal? You need to have your typical shonen MC mentality from now on anon.

62451331
>>62451296
I guess it doesn't really matter. I have to turn my life around somehow anyway.
But the raw hatred pouring out at me from this thread, my God.

62451339
you have two options, one is to find a way to move to a third world (classical sense, not total shithole with no food) country that doesn't already have a tourist scene and teach English for about half of what a licensed professional would earn. the other is to day trade and hopefully snowball that money into something usable

62451345
>>62451269
How did you spend a typical day for the past 15 years? Did you develop any unique skills or creative hobbies that could be leveraged into potential employment?

62451382
>>62450851
OP is right

62451403
>>62451345
Mostly videogames and books. After my Internet got cut off by my mother after an argument I just kept to my room a awhile and apparently fell into a deep depression. My early attempts to get a job flopped for the most part and since I'm miles away from town with no car or driver's license that was kinda it. There's no jobs in the middle of the woods at the end of a freeway.
And then Dad got sick so I had to help Mom take care of him until he died, and now my mother is nearly as bad off as my dad was so we need either a live-in nurse or to put her in a nurse home, because I can't take care of her by myself.

62451415
>>62451331
People hold on dearly to doing what is considered the normal path in the world is. They fight it like life or death because it is what their entire psyche grasps onto for safety. They get comforted by putting down anyone who doesn’t align to that path. They rebel at the concept a deadbeat 40 year old could still work and get lucky enough to get ahead of them, or an alcoholic who fails out of high school could sober up and start a wildly successful business. The concepts of grace and redemption are against their worldview that they alone steered their life how it “should” be

62451426
>>62449961
> 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

I'm a neet who occasionally gets bored and decide to work regular jobs.

I come into the interview drunk and my resume is 100% made up because I tend to quit these jobs after a few weeks.

Literally just lie and make shit up that lines up with where you are trying to get hired.

62451507
>>62451403
Damn that sucks, but like I said earlier now that you have nothing it's a great time to leave your country and to travel, get a backpack and start trekking

62451523
>>62451041
Whoever facilitated OP's deadly inertia really fucked him over.

62451539
>>62451523
There are people who have worked all their lives who still have nothing lmao, maybe you're one of them

62451705
For added, free bullshit, I have several teeth with severe cavities. Some have broken off and the gums grew back over them. One is basically hollow.

62451718
>>62451403
You could leverage this experience with your parents into being a personal care aid or home health aide for other sick and elderly patients. These jobs often struggle to find local workers and have to hire international workers after proving that they could not find a local to take the job. Even rural areas and full of old sick boomers and its growing.

62451719
>>62451331
Truth you don't want to hear isn't hatred. Learn the difference.

62451728
>>62451705
You're in California, apply for every single possible free service they have and physically go into a government center to get someone to set you up. Just spam apply to absolutely everything and make up Mcdonald's tier work history since they'll almost never call references (even better if you have a friend to lie for you though). Aggressively save every penny when you get a job and you'll be alright. You need to try to force yourself to be as optimistic as possible during the entire process and be comfortable with stretching the truth or coming up with a story about what you were doing, everyone does this.

62451729
>>62451718
Great advice, some of these positions include a dental plan I believe

62451733
>>62451705
Which I just looked up and could cost thousands of dollars. Medical night not cover it either.
>>62451718
The reason no one wants that job is because it sucks. Have you ever changed an adults diapers? I would rather eat buckshot then do that again. Besides, I don't think it pays more than a grocery store cashier anyway.

62451737
>>62451733
You've already done it and have experience in it, honestly this seems like the natural way for you, it's like life has been preparing you to deal with shit, adult shit

62451741
>>62451729
Oh good God, are you serious? I can't do the job anyway.

62451742
>>62451539
People who have worked their whole lives at least have learned HOW to work and have a resume to prove it. But that is really only the bare minimum. There is much more to a fulfilling life, much of which has nothing to do with work or money, but still arises out of self-discipline, skill-development, and emotionally maturity.

62451745
>>62451705
Dentalfag reporting in.
If you post some pics I can tell you which ones could be saved with a crown and which you should have extracted ASAP

62451748
>>62451705
Forgot to add you need to fix your teeth by any means necessary, even if it means ripping that shit out of your head because if you don't it will kill you slowly and painfully by infection. Just aggressively pursue free state services.

62451816
I like these threads, it makes me feel better about my own life. But OP seriously how did you get to this point? You would think alarm bells would be going off in your head at age 25, 30 etc. that you need to get your shit together, for fuck sakes your parents aren't going to live forever.

62451821
>>62451099
It's thievery
He can't admit to crimes nigga
You dense

62451832
>>62451403
Wow shouldn't have cut internet mom lol

62451837
Healthcare or zogbot
Neither a comfy choice, but assuming healthcare, you can start sweeping floors then move up to some sort of auxiliary nurse, nursing assistant, then nurse eventually. You could reach 6 figs, but the path would take a fair amount of humiliation, wiping floors and boomer feces

62451874
>>62451745
This anon is offering something genuinely good for seemingly free.
I'm just noting that

item.title: VOO and Chill — 187 replies — 44.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 187 • Updated: 2026-07-06 14:16 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62429683
>>62400493
Anon there is nothing wrong with doing VOO and chill if you have time and principal
Going into wildly volatile ETFs can be okay if it’s a satellite account
Just be careful of FOMO and not being risk averse
You sound like you have a good head on your shoulders though
Remember this is money, real money, your money

62430693
VOO anons where you at?

62430902
>>62400493
Contrarian nerds always lose.
Always.
In middle school dating.
Being high school incels.
In college.
At work.
In love.
At investing.
At all times.
Follow the crowd and try to fit in. Everyone doing anything else is a reject loser.

62431131
>>62430902
Who is being the contrarian here though

62431374
VOO is managed by 2 girl bosses, Michelle Louie & Aurélie Denis. So with your 0.03% management fee you're essentially paying for those do-nothing "day in the life of a portfolio manager" videos for daughters of elite sociopaths.

62431402
>>62431374
A small price to pay for a low expense ratio

62431920
>>62431131
Anyone trying to beat VOO over decades.

62431922
>>62431920
Feel like VUG has consistently beaten VOO though

62431971
>>62431922
Contrarian.
I own VUG and don't own VOO tho :)

62432023
>>62431920
fair enough
thoughts on a younger person doing QQQM?

62434054
Anyone have thoughts or realized returns and experiences on VOO + satellite
accounts instead of just VOO

62435372
Was gonna try to go into this DRAM dip but decided to just do more VOO
But idk a higher return ETF still sounds promising and like a no brainer whether it’s SOXX or QQQM but QQQ has so much crossover with VOO it’s just redundant and only good for growth seasons
I guess this is why VOO and Chill exists to just stop thinking

62436303
>>62431920
What about QQQ though

62436357
i boughted my daily VOO
i'll keep dealing the real world like a good goyim in the mean time.

62436478
>>62436357
huzzah brother

62436499
>>62400493
Where my VGT bros at?

62436507
>>62400636
>Yes and I'm sure that used car and maybe a holiday you can buy with it when you're 65 from cashing out all your investments will be great.....
You say this as if the average /biz/ shitcoin gambler is performing better than VOO which is definitely not the case.

62436510
>>62428031
Your IQ is so actually low. Holy shit.

62436538
>>62436510
Do you think DRAM has value, honest question, and for how long - if so, what dip should one wait for
>>62436499
VGT and QQQ are only doing better thanks to tech having such strong performances for years now, correct?
VOO seems safe since you don't know the future, but on the other hand, thinking the future of the market for the next decades wont always be tech focused seems so short sighted it's silly.....maybe I should start DCA into VGT?

62436562
>>62400493
>VOO
it's EVOO for me lads

62438775
Yep bros
Bought some more VOO and am waiting on my DRAM position to go green so I can sell and not have to deal with thematic ETFs no high expense ratios ever again
Simple as

62440169
>>62431374
>VOO is managed by 2 girl bosses
VOO is passively managed. These 2 girls are not like Kathy Wood picking stocks.

62442752
>>62431374
They could boil alive eat grannies alive for all I care as long as they get the money going, what kind of faggot ass train of thought is this. They could become billionaires and I would not give a shit as long as it keeps compounding. "The people making me rich are foids so it makes me icky" is unironically the foidest take I have ever read.
>>62430902
A year ago I started investing for the first time in my life (I'm in my mid 20s) and thought I was very smart and I would just outperform the market 1% profit at a time. After a whole year I only managed to get a net benefit of a 3% after wasting many hours and carrying heavy bags. I learnt my lesson. VOO and chill.

62442988
>>62442752
>They could become billionaires and I would not give a shit as long as it keeps compounding.

The portfolio managers of VOO have 0 control over whether VOO compounds or not. VOO is simply an ETF that tracks the S&P 500. Their only job is to make sure VOO follows the S&P 500. Once the fund is set up, there really is not much for them to do. I guess if they haven't automated removing and adding companies to the composition of VOO, then they do that.

62444521
Let’s all say thank you to Jack Bogle

62444531
>>62400515
and yet boomers hold 90% of the wealth and growing...

62445651
>>62421799
>5+ yr 60% drawdown event happens
>buy cheaper voo

62446571
>>62444521
thanks chad bogle

62447653
>>62445651
Kek it really is that simple

62450991
>>62446571
Thanks Chad Bogle!

item.title: Now that I've made it I'm wondering if it's worth dumping $6,000 on having a foursome with 3 high class STD-tested escor… — 24 replies — 28.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Now that I've made it I'm wondering if it's worth dumping $6,000 on having a foursome with 3 high class STD-tested escor…

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-07-06 17:38 UTC

OP

Now that I've made it I'm wondering if it's worth dumping $6,000 on having a foursome with 3 high class STD-tested escorts.

Surely there's other rich anons here that have played out that fantasy? How was it? Anything you'd do differently if you had to do it again?

This is financial advice since although I won't miss that amount of money on a once-in-a-lifetime night I still want to get my money's worth.

Early replies (first 3)

62450611
Nigga thinks he's in a porn video lmao

62450618
>>62450597
Do you live in brazil or something? Three "high class" escorts in the US would rape you for 4k a piece

62450623
>>62450611
Yeah pretty much lol but keep in mind I'm 40 so I've had my time to think about what it is I really want in life before my time is up and this is one of those. Like, I know it's not going to be like in a porn video I'm not naive but I'm hoping to at least experience some of the acts only possible with three other women.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62450626
>>62450618
Not Brazil but also not US. Yeah, I checked and in the US it's quite a bit more expensive but I think pretty much everything is more expensive in the US right now for obvious reasons lol.

62450640
>>62450626
Go ahead bro but honestly you'd have more fun just fucking their holes all night one by one instead of having some awkward foresome where you coom in 15 minutes then sit there with 3 coke addicted whores sitting in your place making small talk

62450814
>>62450640
>where you coom in 15 minutes
Don't worry I have my pharma game down so I'm sure that won't be the case. Besides I'm fine with watching for a while at any rate.

62450815
>>62450597
net worth?

62450816
>>62450640
Also I've done enough "one-by-one" in my life that I'm specifically not interested in that. The fantasy is more than one but might as well go big hence three more than one.

62450820
>>62450815
Mid 7

62450825
the std test will be forged

enjoy hiv, hsv and monkey..pox

62450830
>>62450597
Two women is already conspicuous consumption. Three is egregious. I've has threesomes with two women and it just turns into more work for you, not a joke. Women don't work to please men. >>62450597

62450852
>>62450825
>the std test will be forged
Hope not but is that really a popular thing they do especially if they're asking for so much? A test is like $50 here.

>>62450830
>and it just turns into more work for you
I'm ok with that. In fact I'm hoping for that. Was an amateur athlete in my youth and have kept up my physical fitness. Even now in 1-on-1 most women can't keep up. But enough bragging. Duly noted. Genuinely interested to hear other anons experiences as well.

One thing I'm worried about is if the women aren't interested... Since I'll be paying them what if they're just like dead fish or worse...

62450955
>paying woman to pretend to enjoy having sex with you
>paying three woman to pretend to enjoy having sex with you at the same time
they're all going to be dry as fuck

62451014
>>62450597
I've had a couple of threesomes with high end 10/10s and it's generally not worth it beyond doing it once just to have tried it. It's fucking exhausting and you can only cum a couple of times anyway

62451329
>>62450955
Yeah not gonna lie that would suck... Hate having to re-lube.

>>62451014
>it's generally not worth it
>It's fucking exhausting
Could you elaborate? Genuinely interested in your experience.

62451420
>>62450597
Two is already a lot to manage. Maybe start out with a threesome? If you can handle that go to four
Running out of stamina on three is a real risk even on drugs, I imagine it'd be very disappointing

62451458
>>62450852
Look man, it's your money. I just don't want you to come into it with high expectations and then it falls short. I'm glad I got it out of my system, and I don't regret it, but if I was offered one today if be on the fence. Maybe that's just what being past 30 feels like.

62451484
>>62451458
>I just don't want you to come into it with high expectations and then it falls short.
Thanks. I appreciate it. Truly. I'm just relating my history expectations as well. Did you go through an agency or independent? I'm planning on independent because then I figure they should at least have the experience to know how to actually behave like they're interested and whatnot.

62451490
>>62451420
>Maybe start out with a threesome?
It's a one-and-done for me so I'd rather just go all-out and like I said before I'm already sure I'm physically capable.

62451497
>>62451458
>Maybe that's just what being past 30 feels like.
Off-topic but there was a period of about a year where I didn't work out too much and lost physical fitness I'll never get back. But in your case I'm sure if you run and hit the gym you'll feel much MUCH younger again. I still mog the vast majority of people in their 20s fitness-wise and like I said I just turned 40.

62451571
>>62451329
you should never have to lube for sex anon..

62451780
Just do it, ravage those whores like you would in your dreams, thats what's "making it" is all about right?

62451787
>>62451780
>making it
>paying for sex

wew

62451846
>>62451484
I was approached by the pair of women while at a club on both occasions.

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 112 replies — 25.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 112 • Updated: 2026-07-06 14:01 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62442255
>>62426512
I dca 60% of my savings each month while saving the remaining 40% for potential lower lows as we approach October, at which point I'll start going in with all of my monthly savings and that 40% I've been holding back.

62442503
>>62442227
Maybe not immediately. But without infrastructure, there's nothing to drive long-term value anyway.

62442507
>>62442214
Yup, little by little.

62442511
>>62442507
>>62442228
I dollar cost. THe past few months have been gr8

62442553
>>62442511
Where do you do that

62442563
>>62426512
I am DCAing in

62442650
>>62442503
memecoins pump faster. I made money on Ansem and some Solana gems

62442655
>>62439758
never trust centralised services

62442658
buy btc goys

62442668
>>62442658
oy vey

62443383
>>62442650
Well, that's good for you.
>>62442655
Same. Self-custody first, then I check if the protocol has had multiple audits.

62443384
>>62442658
Do it man!

62443930
>>62442668
Goy

62444179
I'm waiting for October, but if it goes below 55k I'll probably start slurping. Everyone who says crypto I'd dead is retarded. Not ripping = dead is their logic.

62444587
>>62426537
That's never going to happen, max will be around 45K. My plan is to DCA, I got in around 58K, stake a few on babylon, I'm waiting to see if there will be more dump

62444608
>>62444587
You really trust the Babylon protocol? It's my first time looking into it and I see I could earn 0.02+ BTC every month. $1200+ every month sounds much better than sitting in my Ledger.

62444998
>>62444608
really

62445005
>>62426512
I'm selling at 100k. I've had enough of holding and should have sold last year. I wanna get out of this shit, it's too much stress.

62445018
>>62427357
do not pull

62445689
>>62444998
DYOR

62446816
>>62445018
Who's plebs rn?

62447133
>>62445005
regrets?

62447140
Ngmi

62447522
>>62442255
Just keep going

62448616
>>62426537
this is probably correct : (

trump and the political class already made their money off crypto last couple years

62448797
>>62427359
Surely the bottom would be zero, no?

62449904
>>62448797
people like to imagine when theyre on drugs

62450375
Why

62450895
Buy Bitcoin

62450912
>>62426512
Buy mote bitcoin and wait for next halving to dump on the normies.

item.title: House buying — 78 replies — 21.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
House buying

Open thread • Replies: 78 • Updated: 2026-07-06 14:49 UTC

OP

I have $180k sitting in an S&P500 index fund and every normie I meet says I'm making a mistake by not getting a mortgage ASAP because housing will only get more expensive.

What is the /biz/ philosophy on this advice? I'm a rentoid and currently pay only around $1000 a month.

Early replies (first 3)

62447251
>>62447246
>every normie I meet says
You could buy and then the market severely crashes.
Housing shouldn't be a financial asset but that's corporatism for you.

62447257
>>62447251
Yea i guess but the stock market "could" also crash and i lose everything. That's not really insightful advice.

62447265
Lmao you are a rentoid

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62448304
>>62447361
Just know that at any point in time you could be renovicted or kicked out because the landlord (or their family) wants to move in. After they live there for a while, they will increase the rent to the market rate. Then your stuck finding a place at 3x rent.

62448311
>>62447843
>Just shut up and buy my bags, goy

62448426
For $180k you could buy 5 acres of rural land and build at least the envelope of a house and not owe anyone anything.

62448439
>>62447246
do nigresses really goon to this kind of slop? Do they even read?

62448441
>>62448426
$180k would barely get you a livable house in Eastern Europe today.

62448442
>>62447246
>bwwm

>italians
>white

62448454
>>62447823
>fast forward 20 years
wont even take a third of that timeline, 38% of the boomer generation already croaked, look it up if you don't believe me.
We haven't seen much of an impact on the prices yet (well, depends where you live becuz here it already tanked a good 25% from 2022 highs) because it's younger boomies inheriting all the houses from older boomers. And since they are boomers, they know what they got and have enough cash reserves to sit on empty houses for years, waiting for a bounce back who will never come. I know becuz my parents inherited 3 houses the past 5 years and sold only one lol.

62448456
>>62448442
the dude literally is darker than the semi-bleached muttess lol. What is going on in BW heads?

62448596
>>62447246
>because housing will only get more expensive.
Maybe, likely so for big cities that get more job offerings and infinite jeets/immigrants. Rural areas? I am not sure, those might start to drop once boomers die off and zoomers/alphas are too poor and low in numbers and not interested in rural houses.

62448620
>>62448596
Incel rentoids now experts on the housing market, is there anything they dont know?

62448624
>>62448620
I am a proud owner of a commieblock apartment and some fields and forestland that I got from my parents/grandparents.

62448648
>>62448441
Build from scratch be your own contractor

62448659
>>62448624
Thats nice maybe keep out of discussions on America if from commieblock land

62448682
>>62448659
>implying you're in the US
idiot. If we kick the foreigners out you'll be gone first.

62448689
Normies are not getting mortgages because rates are like 6.5% when they would not normally be 3%>.

62448696
>>62448689
Boomers paid 20%

62448701
>>62447286
>2nd house for 425k around 31.
>2nd house is worth 900k now
What happened is their dollars lost half the purchasing power.
BTW they didn't but the house for $425k. They bought it for that plus whatever they pay in interest over time. Which is very likely to be closer to the amount they sold it for later.
It's nice to build equity, but don't be a fool.

62449002
>>62447246
>I have $180k sitting in an S&P500 index fund and every normie I meet says I'm making a mistake by not getting a mortgage ASAP because housing will only get more expensive.
>What is the /biz/ philosophy on this advice? I'm a rentoid and currently pay only around $1000 a month.
I'm in a similar position to you and unless you have a lifestyle reason to need a house it doesn't make financial sense right now IMO. Look at it financially, houses cost probably on the ballpark of 300-350k, let's just assume 325k median in a low to medium cost area, that's a 145k loan, at 6.25% interest rate the annual loss of interest only of 9k on year 1 and 8.9k on year 2 contrasted with an annual net loss currently of 12k a year renting but you also dont have other costs like property taxes, insurance, and maintenance costs that should be baked into rent but are not going to baked into that interest loss of yhe house so add those in too, where i live I'd pay 6k prop tax, 1k insurance, and rule of thumb is to budget 1-3% of home value so 3.25k-9.8k. To build on the case if you have money in the SP500 it has returned 10.5% annually (18.9k) or if you want to play it safe 4.5% in CDs (8.1k). That said, it depends on how long you are looking to buy a house, how risk tolerant you are, and if your landlord wants to increase rents. Run those numbers down a spreadsheet and see what makes sense for you in your area but i have come to the conclusion of letting my money ride in the market for the time being knowing the risk that I could get burned.

62450291
>>62447246

62450516
average home price prior to covid was 4.5 years of salary; now its closer to 6.8 years of salary. prices are too high and is way cheaper to rent for now. i save while renting to buy outright, fuck a mortgage.

62450563
>>62450516

62450591
I know this is biz but everyone here is talking about buying a house as though you have to make money off the decision, I could care less about making money off my house, I bought it because it was a huge lifestyle upgrade and now I live 100x better than when I was renting an apartment, it feels like I finally started my real life.

62450594
>>62447246
Let the boomers deal in the real world. Housing is a terrible investment if there is any risk to your income over the term of the loan. My cousin sold TSLA to put a down-payment on a home $120k. The most money he ever had. Home price was $230k. Turned out the home was a lemon and he had dumped another $80k into repairs. Guess what? He lost the house when he and his wife's winery side hustle failed. $200k given to bank for a couple years being a homeowner. Had he put the money into an index fund he'd be worth half a million by now.

62450595
houses are for living in, not speculation
